fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Connecticut Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity connecticut, how to transfer occupational therapist license to connecticut, occupational therapist compact connecticut, connecticut occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Connecticut DPH — OT Licensure
board.mailing_address: Connecticut Department of Public Health, Practitioner Licensing and Investigations Section, 410 Capitol Ave., MS #12HLS, P.O. Box 340308, Hartford, CT 06134-0308

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education every 2 years

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Connecticut.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create a CT eLicense Portal Account
steps.description: Register for an account on the CT eLicense portal to begin your application process. You will need to provide personal information and set up login credentials.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log into your CT eLicense account and complete the application for Occupational Therapist licensure, ensuring all required fields are accurately filled out and necessary documents are uploaded.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verifying the completion of an accredited occupational therapy program, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Connecticut Department of Public Health.

steps.title: Submit NBCOT Scores
steps.description: Request the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) to send your official certification scores directly to the Connecticut Department of Public Health.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee as part of your online application through the CT eLicense portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Connecticut offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Connecticut DPH — OT Licensure for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state may apply for licensure in Connecticut through endorsement. Submit a completed application to the Connecticut DPH — OT Licensure, along with official license verification from your current state and proof of active NBCOT certification. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Connecticut OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $200. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers occupational therapist practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state may apply for licensure in Connecticut through endorsement. Submit a completed application to the Connecticut DPH — OT Licensure, along with official license verification from your current state and proof of active NBCOT certification. Processing typically takes 2 to 4 Weeks.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Connecticut
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Connecticut, you must submit your application and all required documentation through the CT eLicense portal.

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, navigate to the CT eLicense portal and click on 'Register for an Account' or 'New User Registration'. Follow the prompts to provide your personal details, set up a username and password, and verify your email address to activate your account.
